title: Header Fields, Media Types, Negotiation, JSON, and Runtime Validation · Browser and Node Client Systems · Type Recall
summary: A design-system maintainer reconstructing an offline failure handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for header fields, media types, negotiation, json, and runtime validation, producing representation negotiation and validation boundary. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch representation negotiation and validation boundary after a delayed changed cas…
objectives: Handle field names, repeated lines, combination rules, whitespace, size, and hop-by-hop boundaries by specification. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Use Content-Type for enclosed content and Accept for preferred response media ranges with explicit fallback. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Distinguish representation content coding, message delimitation, content length, and decoded size. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Language Charset Negotiation
steps.instruction:
review case — offline design-system client asks for application/json and receives Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8, Content-Encoding gzip, Vary Accept-Language, and field revision:5. Distinguish metadata layers, bound decoded bytes, and reject a changed value type. Competency probe: Interpret charset and language metadata, negotiation quality values, and Unicode boundaries without locale guesses. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
